74822.subject: 🔷 The Daily: 'Most difficult day in the history of digital credit'
74822.body:
Morgan Stanley files amendments for ETH and SOL ETFs, revealing lowest fees in market | Fidelity launches GENIUS-aligned money market fund for stablecoin issuers | Ethereum could face core development 'funding crisis' within nine months, says former EF contributor | Base targets June 25 mainnet launch for Beryl upgrade and new B20 token standard |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Naga Avan-Nomayo

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Friday 🎉 It's a public holiday in the U.S., but we're still here to keep you updated on the industry that never sleeps.

In today's newsletter, *Strive* CEO *Matt Cole* says the *STRC* and *SATA* slide was due to leverage liquidation rather than a deterioration in fundamentals, *Morgan Stanley* files amendments for its proposed *ETH* and *SOL* ETFs, *Fidelity* launches a *GENIUS* -aligned money market fund, and more.

Meanwhile, *bitcoin* microtransactions now make up 80% ( https://www.theblock.co/post/405340/cryptoquant-bitcoin-micro-transactions-btc?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) of all daily transactions, according to *CryptoQuant*.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*📉 'Most difficult day in the history of digital credit': Strive CEO says leverage liquidation drove STRC and SATA selloff*

Bitcoin ( https://www.theblock.co/post/405406/strive-ceo-strc-sata?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Strive* CEO *Matt Cole* said a leverage-driven liquidation event, not deteriorating fundamentals, caused Thursday's sharp selloff in *Strategy's STRC* and *Strive's SATA* preferred stocks.

* STRC fell to a record low of $82.53, and SATA dropped to $92.88 before both rebounded sharply into the close on strong buying demand.
* Trading activity surged as STRC traded 10.6 million shares and SATA traded 1.57 million shares, both well above their typical daily averages.
* Cole said investors often borrow against digital credit instruments to boost returns, creating the risk of forced selling cascades when prices move lower.
* Strive maintained that its dividend reserves remain intact and said the company is well-positioned to continue executing its SATA strategy.
* Strategy and Strive use STRC and SATA to raise capital for *bitcoin* acquisitions through at-the-mark

74515.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — June 14
74515.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-14-oleh-bezuhlyi-akqxf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmbZ4FC4IQ0mg0XDN2T%2BrWg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3vrdyj7E8K3ck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqe0o02q~q3-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqe0o02q-q3
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: A July 1 MiCA cutoff looms for millions of EU crypto users while a DPRK-linked $36M hack and a UK naval seizure exposing USDT sanctions evasion show that crypto's geopolitical risk is no longer theoretical. 🚨 Millions of EU Crypto Users Face Exchange Cutoff as MiCA Deadline Hits in Days On July 1, 2026, the temporary operating permission that allowed crypto companies to serve EU customers while awaiting a full MiCA license expires — and exchanges, brokers, and wallet services that have not secured authorization must immediately cut off access to European users with no further grace period. CryptoSlate reports that the deadline creates a live operational crisis for ordinary investors who may lose access to their funds, positions, or platforms overnight, with little notice from providers scrambling to reach compliance. Read full story 💀 Humanity Protocol's $36M Hack Linked to Suspected North Korean Hackers Cybersecurity firm Quantstamp has attributed the $36 million breach of Humanity Protocol to suspected North Korean threat actors, identifying a fraudulent phishing email spoofing crypto exchange Bithumb as the attack's primary entry vector. The incident adds another eight-figure sum to the growing ledger of assets stolen by DPRK-affiliated hacking groups, which have systematically targeted blockchain projects to fund state programs operating under international sanctions. More details ⚖️ CFTC No-Action Letter Opens First Legal Path for Digital Commodity Perpetuals in the US The CFTC issued a staff no-action letter that clears a legal runway for perpetual futures contracts on digital commodities to operate within the United States — a product category that has until now existed almost exclusively at offshore venues to sidestep regulatory uncertainty. The guidance provides immediate operational clarity for US-domiciled exchanges and their institutional clients, who have long been unable to offer the perpetual swap structures that dominate global crypto derivatives volumes. Read more 📊 SEC Proposes Reg NMS Rule Changes That Could Reshape Tokenized Stock Trading The SEC has proposed rescinding Rules 611 and 610e of Regulation NMS — the order-protection and access rules that have governed US equity market structure for two decades — in a move with substantial downstream implications for how tokenized st
